Workflow.name: Alert - Disable Account

Variable.name: alertDefinition
Variable.Description: The alertDefinition object that matched the alert.

Variable.name: alertId
Variable.Description: The id of the Alert that triggered the workflow.

Variable.name: project
Variable.Description: The compiled project from the plan

Workflow.Description:
Disable accounts when alert is triggered.
If the alert's targetType is Identity, this will set the Identity inactive.
If the alert's targetType is Link, this will disable the account associated to the link.

Step.name: Build Provisioning Plan
Step.Description: Build the Provisioning plan to disable the correct account

Step:
Matlab:-"Is step me ye action execute karo."
-->2. buildAlertPlan kya karta hai? (Deep Dive) Jaise hi workflow shuru hota hai, uske paas sirf alert ki basic jankari hoti hai (jaise: Alert ID, kis target par alert aaya hai, etc.). Woh jankari abhi sirf text form mein hoti hai, use seedhe disable nahi kiya ja sakta.Yahan par buildAlertPlan action parde ke peeche yeh 3 bade kaam karta hai:-
-->Target ka Pata Lagana (Target Identification): Yeh action alert ke andar ki details ko check karta hai. Woh dekhta hai ki alert ka targetType kya hai: Agar targetType Identity hai: Toh yeh samajh jata hai ki puri ki puri person/employee identity ko hi inactive karna hai. Agar targetType Link hai: Toh yeh samajh jata hai ki kisi specific application ke account (jaise sirf uske Salesforce account) ko target karna hai.
-->Operation (Op) ko Map karna:- Aapne jo argument pass kiya tha (jiski value "Disable" thi), yeh action us instruction ko leta hai aur target account ke upar apply karne ke liye ready karta hai.
-->Blueprint taiyar karna (Provisioning Plan):- Yeh SailPoint ka ek blank Provisioning Plan object banata hai aur usme likhta hai: "Mera target yeh account hai, aur mujhe is par 'Disable' operation chalana hai." -------------------------------------------------- ():-ye value kisko pass kar rahi hai jabki value mila hi nahi? -->ref:alertId ki value is line se aa rahi hai: . Yahan input="true" ka matlab hai ki jab bhi koi Alert is workflow ko start karegā, toh SailPoint ka system khud us Alert ki ID isme daal kar (pass kar ke) bhejega.
-->Haan, ke andar jitne bhi values aur references (ref:) diye gaye hain, woh sab ke sab usi Task/Action ko bheje ja rahe hain jo us Step mein action="..." ke roop mein define hai. Ise ek simple programming function ke roop mein samajhiye:- action="buildAlertPlan" ek Function (Method) hai. ke andar di gayi cheezein us function ke Arguments (Parameters/Inputs) hain. // Ek tarah se backend mein yeh function call ho raha hai:- buildAlertPlan(op, alertId);
3. action="compileProvisioningProject" kaun sa action hai? Yeh bhi SailPoint ka ek core inbuilt action hai. Yeh kya karta hai? Pichle step mein jo plan (raw blueprint) bana tha, yeh use check karta hai. Yeh dekhta hai ki user ke paas kya-kya roles hain, kya policies hain, aur unke mutabik workflow ko aage badhane ke liye ek final execution plan taiyar karta hai jise SailPoint ki bhasha mein Project (ProvisioningProject) kehte hain.
4. action="provisionProject" kaun sa action hai? Yeh SailPoint ka sabse aakhiri aur main action hota hai jo badlavo ko sach mein apply karta hai. Yeh kya karta hai? compileProvisioningProject ne jo Project taiyar kiya tha, yeh action use lekar actual target system (jaise Active Directory, Salesforce, ya koi database) ke paas jata hai aur wahan ke account ko sach mein Disable kar deta hai. Yaani action isi step mein hota hai.
() yaha argument name kya hai,kya ye name kahi define hai? -->
*/////////////////
